evals.prompt: My AI answers customer questions but it is only about 60% accurate. I have 100 labeled question-answer pairs in a JSON file. How do I measure the current quality and improve it?
evals.expected_output: A metric function, data loading with .with_inputs(), Evaluate for baseline measurement, and optimization with BootstrapFewShot or MIPROv2, followed by re-evaluation to verify improvement.

assertions.name: defines_metric
assertions.description: Defines a metric function that takes (example, prediction, trace=None) and returns a score

assertions.name: with_inputs_on_examples
assertions.description: Calls .with_inputs() on dspy.Example objects to mark input fields

assertions.name: uses_evaluate
assertions.description: Uses dspy.Evaluate to measure baseline before optimization

assertions.name: uses_optimizer
assertions.description: Applies BootstrapFewShot or MIPROv2 to optimize the program

assertions.name: verifies_improvement
assertions.description: Re-runs evaluation after optimization to compare baseline vs optimized scores

assertions.name: separate_train_dev
assertions.description: Splits data into training and development sets, does not evaluate on training data

evals.prompt: I need to evaluate my AI that extracts structured data from invoices - it pulls out vendor name, date, amount, and line items. How do I write a good metric for this?
evals.expected_output: A partial-credit metric that scores each extracted field independently and returns a weighted average, handling normalization for string comparison and type differences.

assertions.name: partial_credit_metric
assertions.description: Returns partial credit (float 0-1) rather than binary pass/fail for multi-field extraction

assertions.name: field_level_scoring
assertions.description: Scores individual fields independently rather than requiring all fields to match

assertions.name: handles_normalization
assertions.description: Normalizes strings (strip, lower) before comparison to handle formatting differences

assertions.name: metric_returns_numeric
assertions.description: Metric returns a float or bool, not a string

evals.prompt: I optimized my AI with BootstrapFewShot but the score is stuck at 75%. What should I try next?
evals.expected_output: Escalation path: try MIPROv2 for instruction optimization, check if the metric matches real quality, consider increasing training data, or decompose the task if it is too complex for a single step.

assertions.name: suggests_miprov2
assertions.description: Recommends MIPROv2 as the next optimizer to try after BootstrapFewShot

assertions.name: checks_metric_quality
assertions.description: Suggests validating the metric against manual scores to ensure it measures the right thing

assertions.name: considers_data_quantity
assertions.description: Mentions that more training data may help optimization

assertions.name: considers_task_decomposition
assertions.description: Suggests breaking complex tasks into subtasks if single-step optimization plateaus
